data.title: Fed's Bostic: Economy is in a state of substantial stagnation
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Fed's Bostic: Businesses and households lack confidence in making large investments, the uncertainty ahead is becoming more and more serious, we still have a long way to go in terms of inflation, inflation is still far above the target level, and the economy is in a state of substantial stagnation. (Jinshi)

data.title: OpenSea announces that it will launch NFT trading on Solana chain
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, OpenSea announced that Solana token trading is now online for some closed beta users on OS2, and will be gradually launched to more users in the next few weeks. Currently, tokens can be traded, and NFT trading will be launched later.

data.title: KiloEx: The platform has been suspended and the vulnerability has been contained
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News KiloEx issued a statement on the hacking incident, saying, "KiloEx Vault has been hacked. Urge all partner protocols and platforms to immediately blacklist the address to prevent further malicious activity. The vulnerability has been contained. The team has immediately suspended the use of the platform and is working with security partners to track the flow of funds. A bounty program will be released. A full report is being prepared." 
Previously reported, KiloEx was suspected of being hacked, involving about 7 million US dollars.

data.title: South Korea blocks 14 cryptocurrency exchanges from Apple App Store
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: The Financial Services Commission (FSC) of South Korea announced on April 11 that the apps of 14 crypto exchanges including KuCoin and MEXC were blocked in the Apple App Store due to unregistered operations. Previously, the Google Store had blocked 17 unregistered platforms on March 26.
The FSC said that these platforms are suspected of serving Korean users as overseas unregistered virtual asset operators, posing risks of money laundering and user losses. The Financial Information Analysis Institute (FIU) will continue to promote the banning of websites and applications of such platforms. According to FSC regulations, unreported virtual asset service activities are criminal offenses, punishable by up to five years in prison and a fine of 50 million won (about $35,000). (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Japanese medical services company SBC Medical announced the purchase of 5 BTC
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Japanese medical service company SBC Medical Group Holdings announced that based on the announcement of the purchase of Bitcoin disclosed on February 12, 2025, as of April 14, 2025, it has completed the purchase of 5 BTC (approximately 60 million yen/400,000 US dollars). 
Earlier in February, SBC Medical announced plans to purchase 1 billion yen (about 6.7 million US dollars) worth of Bitcoin to achieve the long-term strategic goal of asset diversification and fighting inflation, which is expected to be completed between February and May 2025.

data.title: SEC Delays WisdomTree and VanEck Spot Bitcoin and Ethereum ETF Physical Trading Decision Until June 3
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has postponed the physical subscription and redemption resolution on WisdomTree and VanEck's spot Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs to June 3, 2025. Physical transactions involve the direct exchange of underlying assets, such as Bitcoin and Ethereum, rather than cash. NYSE Arca and Cboe BZX are exchanges associated with these proposals. (The Defiant)

data.title: Bo Hines: US May Use Tariff Revenue to Buy Bitcoin
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Bitcoin Magazine, Bo Hines, executive director of President Trump's Digital Asset Advisory Committee, said that the United States may use tariff revenue to buy Bitcoin.

data.title: Mantra CEO: OM token recovery is a top priority, but it’s still early days
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Mantra CEO John Mullin responded to community concerns after the sharp drop in OM tokens, saying that the project is driving the recovery of tokens, and the repurchase and destruction plan is still in the early stages of formulation. He emphasized that restoring OM tokens is the top priority, and denied the allegations that the team controls 90% of the token supply and major investors sell, saying that the relevant claims are "groundless" and have issued a community transparency report to clarify.
Mullin pointed out that the current largest holder of OM on the exchange is OKX, accounting for 14% of the circulation, about 130 million. He also mentioned that the $109 million ecological fund established with strategic investors will continue to invest in supporting ecological development. Regarding the recent transfer of 38 million OM to Binance cold wallets, he said it was a return operation after the end of the pledge plan. In addition, Mullin revealed that part of the liquidation came from an unnamed exchange that stopped maintaining collateral positions, triggering chain selling pressure. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Federal Reserve's Bostic Highlights Persistent Inflation Challenges
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Federal Reserve official Bostic has stated that inflation remains significantly above target levels, indicating that there is still a long way to go in addressing inflation concerns. He noted that the economy is experiencing substantial stagnation.

data.title: The number of US crypto lobbying groups has increased sharply, and the industry is worried about resource duplication and reduced efficiency
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News As the US cryptocurrency policy environment becomes increasingly active, more than 12 major lobbying organizations are active in Washington, including the Digital Chamber, Blockchain Association, and Crypto Innovation Council (CCI), and new organizations such as the Solana Policy Institute (SPI) and the National Cryptocurrency Association (NCA) have recently been added.
Although industry leaders generally believe that "there is strength in numbers", some former policy advisers are concerned that multiple organizations have a high degree of overlap in membership, funding, and goals, which may lead to waste of resources and excessive bombardment of policymakers. The Stand With Crypto movement led by Coinbase and the supportive political action committee Fairshake are also promoting policy changes. Last year, it invested more than $100 million to support pro-crypto candidates.
As the new Congress advances a number of key crypto bills (covering market regulation, stablecoins, DeFi, proof of reserves, etc.), leadership changes have occurred in multiple organizations, such as Kristin Smith leaving the Blockchain Association to join the Solana Policy Institute, and Perianne Boring, founder of the Digital Chamber of Commerce, also withdrew from daily management and only retained a board position. (CoinDesk)
